restaurants.name: The Flour Works
restaurants.desc: Pune's most beloved European-style café. Artisan sourdough, specialty coffee, and the best workspace vibes in Baner.

restaurants.name: Spice Kitchen
restaurants.desc: Slow-cooked North Indian classics with legendary Dal Makhani simmered overnight.

restaurants.name: Wan Thai
restaurants.desc: Authentic Thai and Pan-Asian in a moody setting. Best date-night in Baner.

restaurants.name: Dosa Plaza
restaurants.desc: Fast, fresh South Indian comfort food. Their crispy Masala Dosa draws a loyal morning crowd.

restaurants.name: Punjab Da Dhaba
restaurants.desc: Punjabi classics that taste straight from Amritsar. Sunday queues are their badge of honour.

restaurants.name: Biryani Blues
restaurants.desc: Slow-cooked dum biryani sealed with dough. The crust-break aroma is the stuff of legends.

restaurants.name: Wakad Social
restaurants.desc: Wakad's most versatile hangout — café by day, cocktail den after dark.

restaurants.name: Hyderabadi House
restaurants.desc: Most authentic Hyderabadi biryani near IT Park. Haleem Fridays are a ritual.

restaurants.name: Tech Hub Café
restaurants.desc: Purpose-built for IT professionals. Best WiFi, healthy menu, standing desks, zero pressure.

restaurants.name: Mithas Sweets
restaurants.desc: Traditional mithai made fresh every morning. Kaju Katli sells out by noon on weekends.

restaurants.name: Aundh Kitchen
restaurants.desc: Authentic Maharashtrian flavours that taste like grandma made them. Sunday thali books out.

restaurants.name: Pizza Republic
restaurants.desc: Pune's best thin-crust wood-fired pizza. 72-hour ferment, imported Italian flour.

restaurants.name: Chaat Corner
restaurants.desc: Six different pani puri flavours. Cash only, always packed, always worth it.

restaurants.name: Shabree Restaurant
restaurants.desc: Kothrud's most iconic restaurant. Their Misal Pav is the gold standard — no debate.

restaurants.name: Vaishali
restaurants.desc: Pune's most legendary restaurant since 1958. No visit to Pune is complete without Vaishali's dosa.

restaurants.name: Good Luck Café
restaurants.desc: Pune's beloved Irani café since 1935. The Bun Maska and Irani chai combo is an institution.

restaurants.name: The Kapila
restaurants.desc: Homestyle Maharashtrian cooking at its finest. Their sabudana khichdi on weekend mornings is a ritual.

restaurants.name: Burger Hut
restaurants.desc: Viman Nagar's best smash burgers. Thin patties, caramelised onions, and sauces that make a mess worth making.

restaurants.name: The Sassy Spoon
restaurants.desc: Viman Nagar's chicest brunch spot. Instagram-beautiful plating and flavours that justify the price tag.

restaurants.name: Malaka Spice
restaurants.desc: KP's most iconic Southeast Asian kitchen since 1997. Rendang and mango smoothies are legendary.

restaurants.name: Arthur's Theme
restaurants.desc: Pune's most theatrical dining experience with live jazz and a menu that matches the opulence.

restaurants.name: Café Peter
restaurants.desc: Camp's beloved heritage café since 1942. Iconic chicken patties and mutton cutlets that generations swear by.

restaurants.name: Royal China
restaurants.desc: Kalyani Nagar's most refined Chinese restaurant. Executive chef trained in Hong Kong, authentic dim sum.

restaurants.name: Smoke House Deli
restaurants.desc: The buzzy all-day café with artisan sandwiches, single-origin coffee, and one of KN's best brunches.

restaurants.name: Prems Graama Bhojanam
restaurants.desc: Authentic village-style Tamil cooking. Banana leaf meals at lunch are a weekly ritual for hundreds of Puneites.

restaurants.name: Mainland China
restaurants.desc: The national chain's Pune outpost does consistent, quality Chinese. Dim sum brunch on Sunday is great value.

restaurants.name: The Flour Box
restaurants.desc: Hadapsar's secret bakery that the east Pune crowd fiercely protects. Daily fresh bakes and specialty coffee.

restaurants.name: Biryani Park
restaurants.desc: Hadapsar's biryani institution. Long queues at lunch are a testament to the authentic recipe.

restaurants.name: PCMC Dhaba
restaurants.desc: PCMC's most popular dhaba-style restaurant. Generous portions, real tandoor, and prices that make sense.

restaurants.name: PCMC Misal
restaurants.desc: PCMC's answer to the great Pune misal debate. Their kat (gravy) has a devoted following in Pimpri-Chinchwad.

restaurants.name: Café Goodluck
restaurants.desc: One of Pune's most legendary breakfast spots. The line at 8AM is just part of the ritual.

restaurants.name: Shabari
restaurants.desc: Authentic Mangalorean and Kerala coastal food tucked in Kothrud. Their Neer Dosa and Prawn Ghee Roast combination has a devoted following.

restaurants.name: Elephant & Co.
restaurants.desc: One of KP's most beloved all-day hangouts. The rooftop terrace on a Pune winter evening is an experience regulars call their happiest weekly ritual.

restaurants.name: Swig
restaurants.desc: KP's most buzzing cocktail bar. The Peri Peri Fries and loaded nachos are designed to be eaten with drinks.

restaurants.name: The Permit Room
restaurants.desc: South Indian food reimagined through a cocktail bar lens. Their Filter Kaapi cocktail and Kerala Beef Fry is one of KP's most talked-about orders.

restaurants.name: Café Mezzuna
restaurants.desc: KP's elegant Italian patio experience. Live jazz on weekends and an Aglio e Olio that might be the best in the city.

restaurants.name: Hard Rock Café
restaurants.desc: The global rock institution with Pune energy. Memorabilia from legends cover every wall and live music nights are genuinely electric.

restaurants.name: Kayani Bakery
restaurants.desc: The most legendary bakery in Pune since 1955. Shrewsbury biscuits sell out — no exceptions. Open till 1PM only.

restaurants.name: George Restaurant
restaurants.desc: Since 1937, George has been the Continental anchor of Camp. Their Caramel Custard and Fish & Chips have been ordered by every generation.

restaurants.name: Dorabjee's Deli
restaurants.desc: Since 1878, Pune's most beloved heritage deli. Cold cuts, imported cheese and freshly baked bread remain unmatched.

restaurants.name: Place of Origin
restaurants.desc: Regional Indian cuisines from across the country under one roof — from Assamese pork to Chettinad chicken.

restaurants.name: Café Sunrise
restaurants.desc: The most beloved morning café on Deccan Gymkhana. Their Misal Pav has been Pune's morning ritual for generations.

restaurants.name: Malwani Mattha
restaurants.desc: Authentic Malwani coastal cooking where the crab masala makes you forget the outside world.

restaurants.name: Cream Centre
restaurants.desc: The vegetarian institution that has satisfied families across generations. Their sizzlers are legendary.

restaurants.name: Bedekar Tea Stall
restaurants.desc: Since 1921, the most legendary chai stall in all of Pune. Standing room only — and nobody complains.

restaurants.name: Chitale Bandhu
restaurants.desc: The most iconic mithai shop in Pune since 1950. Their Bakarwadi is the definitive version and is shipped worldwide.

restaurants.name: Ministry of Crab
restaurants.desc: The award-winning Sri Lankan seafood restaurant now in Pune. Book weeks ahead for weekends.

restaurants.name: SodaBottleOpenerWala
restaurants.desc: The beloved Irani café concept — equal parts heritage and fun. Berry Pulao and Sali Boti are the stars.

restaurants.name: Fog & Fennel
restaurants.desc: Baner's most photogenic café — botanical walls, single-origin coffee and a brunch menu that earns its Instagram fame.

restaurants.name: House of Mandarin
restaurants.desc: Wakad's most serious Chinese kitchen. Proper dim sum handmade every morning.

restaurants.name: Gong
restaurants.desc: Wakad's sleekest Pan-Asian dining room. Low lighting, curated cocktails and a menu that moves from Japanese to Chinese to Southeast Asian.

restaurants.name: Dessert Stories
restaurants.desc: Baner's dessert destination. The chocolate lava cake comes tableside and the Instagram value is real.

restaurants.name: Hotel Shreyas
restaurants.desc: Deccan's famous vegetarian Maharashtrian restaurant. The Thalipeeth with white butter is pure Pune soul food.

restaurants.name: Nimkar Tea Stall
restaurants.desc: The original kulhad chai spot of old Pune. Standing beside regulars who've been coming for 30 years is humbling.

restaurants.name: The Biryani Shop
restaurants.desc: Hadapsar's most dedicated biryani kitchen. The Lamb Dum Biryani is slow-cooked for hours and worth every minute.

restaurants.name: The Breakfast Club
restaurants.desc: The definitive all-day breakfast spot in Aundh. Bottomless coffee, eggs cooked exactly as ordered.

restaurants.name: The Fisherman's Wharf
restaurants.desc: Coastal Maharashtrian and Goan seafood executed so well that regulars come from across the city.

restaurants.name: Smoke & Spice
restaurants.desc: Pune's most serious BBQ kitchen. Their 14-hour Smoked Brisket tastes exactly like that much effort.

restaurants.name: Chulha Chauka
restaurants.desc: Rustic home-style Rajasthani cooking. Weekend Dal Baati Churma is something genuinely special.

restaurants.name: The Daily Grind
restaurants.desc: Third-wave coffee done right in IT Park. Single-origin pour-overs, fast WiFi, and enough power outlets for the entire team.

restaurants.name: Shabree Sinhgad
restaurants.desc: The Sinhgad Road outpost of the iconic Shabree brand. Same uncompromising Misal Pav, same fiercely loyal breakfast crowd.

restaurants.name: Hotel Sinhgad Vaishali
restaurants.desc: The Sinhgad Road institution for South Indian comfort food. Their Filter Coffee is legitimately great.

restaurants.name: Panchali
restaurants.desc: Sinhgad Road's best unlimited Maharashtrian thali — home-style cooking with generous portions.

restaurants.name: Cafe Indulge
restaurants.desc: A cosy neighbourhood café that Sinhgad Road residents treat as their living room. Reliable cold brew and honest sandwiches.

restaurants.name: Dhaba 59
restaurants.desc: The late-night dhaba that Sinhgad Road residents rally around. Open till 1AM on weekends — the Dal Tadka at 11PM hits different.

restaurants.name: Sinhgad Fort View
restaurants.desc: The café at the base of Sinhgad Fort road with the view that makes everything taste better.

restaurants.name: Thai Thali
restaurants.desc: KP's most authentic Thai kitchen. Hand-pounded curry pastes and a Green Curry that makes you close your eyes.

restaurants.name: Bangkok Bites
restaurants.desc: Viman Nagar's go-to for late-night Thai. The Pad Thai is textbook-perfect and the Tom Yum soup packs a punch.

restaurants.name: Lemongrass
restaurants.desc: Pune's most celebrated Thai restaurant. The Massaman Curry is slow-braised and genuinely complex.

restaurants.name: Tuk Tuk
restaurants.desc: A hidden Thai gem in KN with a street-food vibe. Their Som Tam papaya salad is dangerously addictive.

restaurants.name: Sakura
restaurants.desc: Pune's best Japanese. Omakase-style sashimi platter and hand-rolled sushi that rival Mumbai's finest.

restaurants.name: Ramen Republic
restaurants.desc: 18-hour pork bone broth, wavy noodles, and a soft egg. The Tonkotsu Ramen here is a religious experience.

restaurants.name: Izakaya
restaurants.desc: A proper Japanese gastropub. Pan-fried gyoza, cold Sapporo, and yakitori skewers done exactly right.

restaurants.name: Nori
restaurants.desc: A quiet neighbourhood sushi bar that KN regulars guard jealously. The Dragon Roll is a showstopper.

restaurants.name: Coastal Kitchen
restaurants.desc: The best coastal seafood in Pune. Butter garlic crab flown in from Goa and Malvani fish curry that's pure soul.

restaurants.name: Goa Shack
restaurants.desc: Goan beach shack vibes in the middle of Baner. Their Prawn Recheado masala is the real deal.

restaurants.name: Bombil & Beyond
restaurants.desc: Camp's best-kept seafood secret. Crispy Bombil fry and Surmai curry cooked exactly how old Pune families like it.

restaurants.name: The Lobster Trap
restaurants.desc: Pune's premium seafood destination. Lobster Thermidor for special occasions, Grilled Pomfret for every Tuesday.

restaurants.name: Casa Mexico
restaurants.desc: KP's liveliest Mexican joint. Massive burritos, loaded nachos, and margaritas that hit the right spot.

restaurants.name: Taco Town
restaurants.desc: Baner's taco specialists. The crispy fish tacos with mango salsa are genuinely craveable.

restaurants.name: El Rancho
restaurants.desc: A colourful Mexican cantina with the best quesadillas in town and a guacamole made fresh tableside.

restaurants.name: The Grille Room
restaurants.desc: KP's finest steakhouse. Dry-aged cuts, classic French sauces, and a wine list that does justice to the food.

restaurants.name: Bistro Bleu
restaurants.desc: A charming French bistro with crispy croque monsieurs, proper French onion soup, and crème brûlée done right.

restaurants.name: The Brasserie
restaurants.desc: KN's most elegant all-day dining. The weekend brunch is a Pune institution — Eggs Benedict with smoked salmon.

restaurants.name: Fork & Knife
restaurants.desc: A cosy neighbourhood European eatery doing honest pasta, wood-fired chicken and proper tiramisu.

restaurants.name: Pit Stop BBQ
restaurants.desc: Baner's BBQ powerhouse. The 6-hour smoked pork ribs fall off the bone and come with killer coleslaw.

restaurants.name: Smokehouse Deli
restaurants.desc: The benchmark for BBQ in Pune. Their smoked brisket platter with three sides is the kind of meal you plan your week around.

restaurants.name: Coal & Fire
restaurants.desc: Wakad's late-night BBQ spot. Charcoal-grilled wings in 6 sauces and cold beer — exactly what the weekend calls for.

restaurants.name: The Grill House
restaurants.desc: Hinjewadi's favourite post-work grill. Mixed platters with seekh kebab, wings and corn hit the spot after a long day.

restaurants.name: Bread & Butter
restaurants.desc: Pune's best croissants, full stop. Laminated to golden perfection every morning from 7AM. Get there early.

restaurants.name: The Oven
restaurants.desc: KP's artisan bakery with a sourdough starter that's been alive for 5 years. Weekend loaves sell out by 9AM.

restaurants.name: Crumbs & Co
restaurants.desc: A tiny neighbourhood bakery that Aundh residents start their mornings with. The giant cinnamon rolls are genuinely great.

restaurants.name: Flour Power
restaurants.desc: KN's cult-status patisserie. The Kouign-Amann is criminally underrated and the eclairs are flawless.

restaurants.name: Pumpernickel
restaurants.desc: Camp's old-school European bakery. German rye, seeded baguettes and proper butter cookies that feel like 1985.

restaurants.name: Green Bowl
restaurants.desc: Baner's most popular health-food café. The Acai Bowl with granola and fresh fruit is worth every calorie (or lack thereof).

restaurants.name: The Salad Bar
restaurants.desc: Pune's first serious plant-based kitchen. Build-your-own Buddha Bowls with 25 toppings and excellent cold-pressed juices.

restaurants.name: Earthen Cafe
restaurants.desc: Kothrud's organic, plant-forward café doing millets, ragi and zero-waste cooking. The Ragi Dosa with sambhar is exceptional.

restaurants.name: Nirvana Vegan
restaurants.desc: The jackfruit burger that converted Pune's meat lovers. Creative plant-based comfort food that doesn't feel like a compromise.

restaurants.name: Seoul Kitchen
restaurants.desc: Pune's first proper Korean BBQ experience. Grill-at-table with banchan, ssamjang and cold Hite beer.

restaurants.name: K-Pop Bites
restaurants.desc: Viman Nagar's Korean street food café. Bibimbap, Korean fried chicken and ramyeon done with real care.

restaurants.name: Kimchi House
restaurants.desc: A small, serious Korean kitchen in Baner where homesick Koreans from the IT park come for comfort food.

restaurants.name: Olive Garden
restaurants.desc: An alfresco Mediterranean escape in KP. Mezze platters, wood-fired flatbreads, and Lebanese wine under fairy lights.

restaurants.name: Hummus & Co
restaurants.desc: Baner's Israeli-Mediterranean café. The Shakshuka is the best in Pune and the hummus is made fresh every hour.

restaurants.name: The Pita Place
restaurants.desc: KN's under-the-radar Mediterranean spot. Crispy falafel wraps, fattoush salad and baklava that's flown in from Dubai.

restaurants.name: Vada Pav Corner
restaurants.desc: The vada pav that Shivajinagar office-goers have eaten every day for 20 years. Dry garlic chutney is the secret weapon.

restaurants.name: Bun Maska Studio
restaurants.desc: Camp's most photogenic Irani café snack stop. Bun Maska with thick Irani chai — the original Pune power breakfast.

restaurants.name: Chaat Chowk
restaurants.desc: Deccan's most beloved chaat counter. The pani puri water is pure Mumbai-style and the bhel puri is perfectly assembled.

restaurants.name: Kanda Bhaji Stall
restaurants.desc: The kanda bhaji stall that old Pune swears by. Served piping hot in newspaper, best eaten in the rain.

restaurants.name: Dabeli Den
restaurants.desc: Authentic Kutchi Dabeli in Kothrud — sweet, spicy, tangy all at once. The peanut and pomegranate topping makes it.

restaurants.name: Pizza Primitivo
restaurants.desc: Neapolitan-style pizza with 00 flour dough and a wood-fired oven that hits 450°C. The Margherita is a revelation.

restaurants.name: Slice of Heaven
restaurants.desc: New York-style pizza by the slice in Baner. Massive, foldable slices with quality toppings and proper mozzarella.

restaurants.name: Dough & Co
restaurants.desc: Viman Nagar's artisan pizza bar. The truffle and mushroom pizza with fresh burrata is the kind of thing you dream about.

restaurants.name: Rustica
restaurants.desc: A cosy neighbourhood pizzeria doing honest Italian. The Funghi pizza with four mushrooms and rosemary oil is consistently great.

restaurants.name: Smash Bros
restaurants.desc: Pune's smash burger obsession starts here. Crispy caramelised edges, American cheese, brioche bun — pure fast food elevated.

restaurants.name: The Patty Lab
restaurants.desc: KP's gourmet burger destination. A Wagyu beef patty with truffle mayo and caramelised onions on a sesame brioche.

restaurants.name: Big Bun Theory
restaurants.desc: Viman Nagar's crispy chicken burger champion. Buttermilk-fried chicken, pickles, and Nashville hot sauce on a potato bun.

restaurants.name: Stack'd
restaurants.desc: Wakad's late-night burger fix. Massive, messy, brilliant BBQ bacon burgers with hand-cut fries.

restaurants.name: Scoop Shop
restaurants.desc: KP's most beloved ice cream parlour. 40 flavours from matcha to Alphonso mango — the queue is always worth it.

restaurants.name: Mithai Magic
restaurants.desc: Old Pune's most treasured mithai shop. The Puran Poli with ghee is the dessert that Pune was built on.

restaurants.name: Waffles & More
restaurants.desc: Viman Nagar's dessert café obsession. Loaded Belgian waffles with Nutella, fresh berries, and hand-churned vanilla ice cream.

restaurants.name: Cheesecake Factory
restaurants.desc: Baner's dessert destination for serious cheesecake. 12 varieties daily — the classic NY baked with berry compote is perfect.

restaurants.name: Biryani Blues
restaurants.desc: The Hyderabadi biryani that KP swears by. Slow-dum cooked in clay pots with saffron and kewra — the fragrance hits from the street.

restaurants.name: Dum Pukht
restaurants.desc: Shivajinagar's biryani royalty. The mutton dum biryani is sealed with dough and opened tableside — every bite is theatre.

restaurants.name: Paradise Biryani
restaurants.desc: Hadapsar's beloved biryani joint serving over 500 plates daily. The chicken biryani is consistent, affordable, and always satisfying.

restaurants.name: Peshawri
restaurants.desc: Pune's finest frontier cuisine. Dal Bukhara simmered for 18 hours, buttery naan, and tandoori dishes that belong in Delhi.

restaurants.name: Punjabi Tadka
restaurants.desc: Wakad's most satisfying Punjabi kitchen. Winter special Sarson ka Saag with white butter and makki roti — genuinely done right.

restaurants.name: Frontier
restaurants.desc: Camp's most dramatic North Indian kitchen. The whole-leg Raan slow-roasted in the tandoor is an occasion in itself.

restaurants.name: Idli House
restaurants.desc: Kothrud's idli institution. The softest, fluffiest idlis in Pune served with 4 chutneys and sambar that has actual depth.

restaurants.name: Chettinad House
restaurants.desc: Authentic Chettinad cooking in Baner. The fiery, aromatic Chettinad chicken curry with parotta is unlike anything else in Pune.

restaurants.name: Madras Café
restaurants.desc: Hadapsar's authentic Tamil breakfast spot. Filter coffee in a steel tumbler-davara and crispy ghee-roast dosa that's earned its following.

restaurants.name: Third Wave Coffee
restaurants.desc: Baner's most serious specialty coffee bar. Single-origin beans, dialled-in espresso, and a cortado that converts non-coffee drinkers.

restaurants.name: The Reading Room
restaurants.desc: Deccan's quietest, most bookish café. Floor-to-ceiling shelves, slow service, and a peace that's increasingly rare in Pune.

restaurants.name: Café Parizad
restaurants.desc: The Irani café that defines Camp's soul. Same chai, same wooden chairs, same beautiful chaos — since 1955.

restaurants.name: Artisan Roast
restaurants.desc: KN's specialty coffee destination. 72-hour cold brew, Kenyan single-origin pour-overs and fresh pastries from their own kitchen.

restaurants.name: Terrace Talk
restaurants.desc: Hinjewadi's rooftop café with Pune skyline views. Avocado toast, specialty coffee, and a sunset that makes the IT grind bearable.

restaurants.name: Aaswad Upahara
restaurants.desc: Pune's most celebrated Maharashtrian snack house. The Sabudana Khichdi is the gold standard — light, fragrant, never sticky.

restaurants.name: Poona Guest House
restaurants.desc: Old Camp's Maharashtrian home-cooking secret. The stuffed Bharli Vangi with groundnut masala is everything a grandmother would make.

restaurants.name: Chitale Bandhu
restaurants.desc: The iconic Chitale Bandhu outlet on Deccan. The Bhakarwadi that Pune exports to the world and the Bakarwadi that converts outsiders.

restaurants.name: Kolhapuri Kitchen
restaurants.desc: The fiery Kolhapuri red mutton curry (Tambada Rassa) that makes your eyes water and your heart sing. Served with bhakri.

restaurants.name: Mainland China
restaurants.desc: KP's most upscale Chinese dining. The Peking duck carved tableside is a ceremony and the dim sum basket is always fresh.

restaurants.name: Wok Express
restaurants.desc: Hinjewadi's fastest Chinese lunch fix. Build-your-own wok bowl — schezwan noodles with extra chilli oil is the IT crowd's go-to.

restaurants.name: Dragon Garden
restaurants.desc: Aundh's neighbourhood Chinese restaurant that's been packing tables for 15 years. The Kung Pao Chicken is perfectly balanced.
